steps.title: Recall that the y-intercept occurs where x = 0
steps.content: The y-intercept is the point where a line crosses the y-axis. This always happens when $x = 0$. We need to find the y-coordinate of that point.

steps.title: Read the y-value from the graph
steps.content: Looking at the graph, trace up the y-axis to find where the line intersects it. At $x = 0$, the line crosses the y-axis at $y = 2$. Therefore, the y-intercept is the ordered pair $(0, 2)$. That is the correct answer.
